lines.en_lit: southeast was the chamber in which the shewbread was prepared. northeast in which the Hasmoneans sequestered the altar stones that were desecrated by the gentile kings northwest in which descended to the Hall of Immersion. Rav Huna said: Who taught Middot Rabbi Eliezer ben Ya’akov,

As we learned women’s courtyard were a length of 135 by a width of 135 and there were four chambers in its four corners. And what serve? southeast was the Chamber of the Nazirites, as there the nazirites cook their peace-offerings and shave their hair and cast beneath the pot northeast was the Chamber of the Woodshed, where blemished priests, stand and examine the logs by worms, as any log in which there are worms is disqualified for on the altar.

northwest was the Chamber of the Lepers, southwest Rabbi Eliezer ben Ya’akov said: I forgot what would serve. Abba Shaul says: They would place wine and oil there and it was called the Chamber of the House of Oils.

So too, it is reasonable Rabbi Eliezer ben Ya’akov, as we learned All the walls that were there were high except for the Eastern Wall, as the priest who burns the heifer stands on the Mount of Olives, and directs and looks toward the entrance of the Sanctuary when sprinkles the blood.

And we learned All the entrances that were there twenty cubits high and ten cubits wide. And we learned Inside a latticed gate. And we learned Inside the rampart, ten cubits there were twelve stairs; stair half a cubit high and half a cubit deep,

fifteen stairs ascend from within descend from the Israelite courtyard to the women’s courtyard. stair half a cubit high and half a cubit deep, And we learned between the Entrance Hall and the altar twenty-two cubits and there were twelve stairs stair half a cubit high and half a cubit deep,
And we learned Rabbi Eliezer ben Ya’akov says: There was stair there one cubit high, and the platform was placed upon it and on it three stairs, each half a cubit,
Granted, if you say Rabbi Eliezer ben Ya’akov, that is the entrance was concealed.
However, if you say the Rabbis, isn’t there half a cubit through which the entrance can be seen?

Rather, not conclude from it Rabbi Eliezer ben Ya’akov? Rav Adda bar Ahava said: Rather, whose is that It is Rabbi Yehuda, as it was taught Rabbi Yehuda says: The altar is centered and stands in the middle of courtyard, it was thirty-two cubits
